---
pagetitle: "FETP India business case | Research | Mesfin Genie"
description: "A World Bank-commissioned business case for scaling up Field Epidemiology Training Programmes in India, supported by the STEPS decision aid."
title-block-style: none
page-layout: full
image: /assets/og-card.png
---

```{=html}
<nav class="breadcrumb" aria-label="Breadcrumb"><a href="/">Home</a> / <a href="/research/">Research</a> / <span aria-current="page">FETP India business case</span></nav>
<header class="page-header"><div class="page-shell"><p class="eyebrow">Project</p><h1>Upscaling FETP in India</h1></div></header>
<section class="content-section"><div class="page-shell narrow">
<h2>The problem</h2>
<p>Scaling up Field Epidemiology Training Programmes (FETP) strengthens a country's capacity to detect and respond to disease, but scale-up must be planned within a budget and justified with a clear business case.</p>
<h2>Research questions</h2>
<p>How should FETP scale-up in India be prioritised and planned so that cost, stakeholder endorsement, and expected health benefit are balanced?</p>
<h2>Role, team, and partner</h2>
<p>Commissioned by the World Bank Group. Dr Mesfin Genie contributed to the business case and led the supporting decision aid, working with M. Wu, J. Henriquez, A. Gebremariam, A. Melia, and collaborators.</p>
<h2>Methods and data</h2>
<p>A discrete choice experiment with stakeholders, combined with cost templates and epidemiological multipliers, underpins a transparent planning model.</p>
<h2>Outputs</h2>
<ul class="cv-list"><li><a href="/tools/steps/">STEPS FETP Scale-up Decision Aid</a></li></ul>
<h2>Funding and status</h2>
<p>Commissioned research for the World Bank Group (International Bank for Reconstruction and Development), 10 March 2025 to 31 December 2025.</p>
<h2>Related resources</h2>
<ul class="cv-list"><li><a href="/tools/steps/">STEPS decision aid</a></li></ul>
</div></section>
```
